title: Stage Management Assistant – Temp (1.5months)
description:
We are seeking a Temporary Stage Management Assistant to ensure the smooth flow of the Youth Model ASEAN Conference (YMAC) Opening and Closing Ceremonies at Singapore Polytechnic’s Convention Centre (SPCC).  The duration is from end of February 2026 to 11 April 2026.  This role is central to managing stage operations, cue sheets, and coordinating entrances and exits, while working closely with the emcee to align live cues during speeches and transitions.

Key Responsibilities

· Develop and maintain cue sheets for all program segments (performances, speeches, panels, presentations).

· Plan and manage stage entrances and exits for performers, speakers, and presenters.

· Work closely with the emcee, responding to cues given during speeches and announcements.

· Ensure seamless transitions between performances, panel discussions, speeches, and presentations.

· Liaise with technical crew (AV, lighting, sound) to synchronize cues with stage actions.

· Oversee timing and flow to keep the ceremony on schedule.

· Maintain stage safety and professional presentation throughout the event.

Required Skills & Attributes

· Minimally worked in 3 previous shows and/or events and in an assistant and/or stage manager role.

· Strong organizational and multitasking skills.

· Clear communication and ability to give concise cues.

· Calm under pressure and quick problem-solving ability.

· Availability for rehearsals and both ceremonies.

Commitment

· No weekend work (except for Sat, 11th April for 3 hours - 8am to 11am)

· Pre-event briefing and rehearsals.

· Full presence during opening and closing ceremonies.

· Post-event debrief and handover notes.

postedCompany.description:
Most Positive Workplace Collaboration Award - Singapore International Chamber of Commerce (SICC) Awards 2025

Ranked #19 for The Straits Times Singapore’s Best Employers 2024 & 2025


Singapore Polytechnic has been training and developing talent since 1957, even before Singapore became independent, fuelling the economic growth of the nation over the many decades. Continuing Education and Training (CET) was given prominence in 1979 in Singapore Polytechnic when Singapore needed well-trained and skilled manpower to boost its push to value-added manufacturing and services.


The Professional & Adult Continuing Education Academy or PACE, launched at Singapore Polytechnic in 2010, provides the focal point for CET – as Singapore Polytechnic continues to build on our success in providing new and effective pathways for learners of all ages to develop their skills and knowledge so as to be employable in an ever-changing workplace.
postedCompany.name: Singapore Polytechnic

districts.location: D05 Clementi New Town, Hong Leong Garden, Pasir Panjang
